purpose: Canonical bounded smoke examples for fresh installation and early tool exploration. Use these examples to prove which tool surfaces really work instead of assuming build success or running services are sufficient.
operatingRules: Prefer the highest-value surfaces you will need soon instead of trying to finish the whole matrix in one turn.
Keep each smoke step bounded: one exec command, one exec-session step, or one browser step per turn.
If a tool surface was not tested yet, say that it is untested instead of implying it works.
Use fresh evidence from this run or from a freshly verified artifact.

surfaces.goal: Verify one-shot bounded shell execution with real stdout, stderr, and exit-code capture.
surfaces.example: Run a harmless bounded command such as `printf 'tool-smoke exec ok\n'` or `pwd` and cite the exact output.

surfaces.goal: Verify a reusable session can start, accept input, return output, and terminate cleanly.
surfaces.example: Start a non-TTY session such as `bash`, send `pwd` or `echo session-ok`, read the output, then terminate the session.

surfaces.goal: Verify the live loop can ingest interrupts and surface the result back into the visible channel.
surfaces.example: Use the real interrupt path when available and confirm the resulting task, event, or visible reply exists in SQLite, the TUI, or the BIOS dialogue.

surfaces.goal: Verify real browser automation, not only package presence.
surfaces.example: Create a tiny local HTML file, open it in the browser, then use `dump_dom`, `screenshot`, or `inspect_visual` and cite the resulting artifact.

surfaces.goal: Verify GUI or browser work targets the active graphical session when live desktop interaction is required.
surfaces.example: Identify the active session through `loginctl`, `DISPLAY`, and session environment evidence before any GUI mutation, and cite the chosen live session.

surfaces.goal: Verify assigned mail or channel tooling in a non-destructive way once credentials and policy exist.
surfaces.example: Run a read-only or draft-safe step such as `--help`, account listing, or draft preview generation. Do not send real mail as part of the smoke.

definitionOfDoneHint: Build success, installed binaries, or active services do not prove an end-to-end tool surface works.
For every claimed working surface, cite fresh smoke evidence.
List passed, failed, and still-untested surfaces explicitly.
